posts.title: "Phil Johnston LinkedIn" (Leaving LinkedIn and Choosing Independence)
posts.excerpt: After nearly two decades on LinkedIn, I lost my account overnight due to a compromise and policy enforcement. I understand LinkedIn's decision, but the experience forced me to confront how fragile a centralized professional identity really is. This post isn't about blame. It's about lessons learned, security realities, and why I'm choosing a more independent path forward.

posts.title: The Future of Micro-Niche AI Tools
posts.excerpt: As someone who's soon to be less relevant when it comes to technology, I am personally augmenting my workflows to include self-developed tools to help me get my work done. One recent example is a quick script that will test server...

posts.title: Encouraging Developers to Share Their Stories in Their Own Words
posts.excerpt: As a developer relations professional, one of our goals is to highlight the voices of the developers we serve.

posts.title: Micro-niche vibe coding?
posts.excerpt: I've been vibing lately with terminal UI (TUI) apps. Small personal tools that help me optimize a very specific task or workflow. I call these "Micro-Niche" tools. I can spin these tools up in an afternoon, where before I would have just kept plodding along without them. This is the project that finally worked!
